source.catalog_ref: I-EU11 (collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Mexico, I-LA thread)

full_prompt: Design the brake pedal of an collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Mexico 2026 car per FS Rules 2026 T.4.8 Brake Pedal, which requires the pedal to withstand a 2000 N applied load (per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2024+ update, raised from 1779 N in older revisions) without yielding and without fracture. Pedal geometry: total lever length 200 mm from pivot axis to pedal pad centerline, pivot axis on a 10 mm steel dowel (12H7 bore through the pedal), pedal pad 80 mm x 50 mm. Pedal machined from 6061-T6 aluminum billet (E 68.9 GPa, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), 10 mm thick along pedal axis, with a reinforcing web running the pedal length 15 mm tall. Master cylinder pushrod attaches to a clevis 60 mm from pivot axis via a 6 mm steel pin. Pedal ratio 200/60 = 3.33. Load cases: (LC1) mandatory collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark qualification: 2000 N applied axially at pad centerline along the primary pedal axis (worst-case direction per T.4.8), reacting at pivot and pushrod clevis; (LC2) lateral slip: 200 N lateral applied at pad while pedal is held at nominal braking force 1000 N (inadvertent lateral input from driver foot); (LC3) fatigue: 0 - 500 N repeated brake application over 1e5 cycles (typical endurance race duty); (LC4) adverse pivot misalignment: pedal binds and reacts 2000 N pad load through only the pushrod clevis at 60 mm while pivot is frictionally locked; (LC5) emergency stall: pedal stuck fully depressed and driver kicks it with 3000 N for 0.5 s (transient overload, FEA as static envelope). Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 mandatory 2000 N case, max von Mises stress must remain below 276 MPa yield AND below 207 MPa (276 / 1.33 yield FoS per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark T.4.8 safety margin); (R2) pedal pad deflection under LC1 must remain below 10 mm (excessive travel wastes brake stroke); (R3) pedal dry mass must not exceed 0.45 kg; (R4) fatigue alternating stress under LC3 must remain below 96 MPa endurance limit for 6061-T6 with 1.3 margin = 74 MPa; (R5) under LC5 overload, pedal must not fracture (max stress < 310 MPa ultimate) but permanent deformation is acceptable; (R6) pushrod-clevis bolt (6 mm steel) shear under LC1 must remain below 250 MPa. Thermal, fluid, radiation, vibration analyses not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of pedal, machining drawing, FEA per load case, fatigue margin, mass.
short_prompt:
Design the brake pedal of a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Mexico 2026 car per FS Rules 2026 T.4.8 Brake Pedal: withstand a 2000 N applied load without yielding and without fracture (2024+ update, up from 1779 N in older revisions).

Envelope: 6061-T6 aluminum billet, 200 mm total lever length pivot-to-pad, 12H7 pivot bore on a 10 mm steel dowel, 80 x 50 mm pad, 10 mm thick along pedal axis with a 15 mm-tall reinforcing web along the length; master-cylinder pushrod clevis at 60 mm from pivot via a 6 mm steel pin. Pedal ratio 200/60 = 3.33.

Factor regime: LC1 must stay below yield 276 MPa AND below 207 MPa (276/1.33 T.4.8 safety margin); pad deflection under LC1 < 10 mm; pedal dry mass <= 0.45 kg; LC3 alternating < 74 MPa (endurance 96 MPa with 1.3 margin); LC5 overload < 310 MPa ultimate (permanent deformation acceptable); pushrod-clevis bolt shear under LC1 < 250 MPa.

Load cases.
- T.4.8 qualification: 2000 N axial at pad centerline along the primary pedal axis, reacting at pivot and pushrod clevis.
- Lateral slip: 200 N lateral at pad while held at nominal 1000 N braking force.
- Fatigue: 0-500 N repeated brake application over 1e5 cycles (endurance-race duty).
- Adverse pivot misalignment: pedal binds and 2000 N reacts only through the 60 mm pushrod clevis (pivot frictionally locked).
- Emergency stall: 3000 N for 0.5 s (static envelope for transient kick).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Endurance-life check LC3 uses FEA stress output in Goodman post-process.

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Mexico 2026 Brake Pedal
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 machined brake pedal for collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark T.4.8 2000 N qualification, 200/60 mm ratio, 1e5 cycle fatigue.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 276 / 1.33 per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark T.4.8

eval_coverage: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark T.4.8 mandatory load case
